extra.reasoning:
The property "globally(opted_out implies not sent)" requires that no reachable state has opted_out=true AND sent=true. Transition [t10] (on send_attempt if opted_out and stage>=2: sent:=true) directly sets sent=true while opted_out is true, so it is the candidate violation. I just need to reach stage>=2 with opted_out=true and then fire send_attempt.

Trace from initial state (opted_out=false, sent=false, stage=0):
1. optout -> [t1] (guard true): opted_out=true, sent=false, stage=0.
2. key_0 -> [t3] (stage==0): stage=1, sent=false; opted_out stays true.
3. key_0 -> [t6] (stage==1): stage=2, sent=false; opted_out stays true.
4. send_attempt -> [t9] guard is (opted_out and stage<2) = false; [t10] guard is (opted_out and stage>=2) = true, so [t10] fires: sent:=true.

Resulting state: opted_out=true, sent=true, stage=2 — opted_out is true but sent is true, violating the property. This occurs at 4 events, within the 5-event bound. Note that opting out (t1) and the key transitions all reset sent to false, and t2 only sets sent when not opted_out; but t10 re-enables sending once stage advances to 2 even though the user opted out, which is the flaw.
